source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just crafting messaging; you’re shaping how the market understands AI-driven procurement. As Product Marketing Manager at Lio, you translate product innovation into compelling stories that drive pipeline, adoption, and category leadership.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Define and refine Lio’s positioning, messaging, and value propositions

 - Translate product features into clear customer outcomes and differentiated narratives

 - Enable Sales with pitch decks, battle cards, case studies, and objection-handling frameworks

 - Lead product launches end-to-end, from narrative to go-to-market execution

 - Conduct market, competitor, and customer research to sharpen our positioning

 - Collaborate closely with Product, Growth, and Sales to align roadmap and messaging




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have 2–5 years of experience in product marketing, B2B SaaS marketing, or a similar role

 - Think strategically but execute hands-on

 - Can simplify complex AI or technical topics into sharp, compelling messaging

 - Understand enterprise buyers and multi-stakeholder sales cycles

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-growth environments




WHY JOIN US?

At Lio, you’ll define how a new category of AI Agents is positioned in the enterprise market. Your messaging will influence strategic deals, product direction, and brand perception. Move fast, own the narrative, and help build a category-defining AI company. Are you in?



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Anika https://www.linkedin.com/in/anika-holinski-2849b4129/!

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

For decades, procurement teams have relied on spreadsheets, emails, and fragmented processes to manage billions of dollars in spend. As AI agents become capable of taking over increasingly complex workflows, the way companies run procurement is about to change.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As a Sales Development Representative, you're not just booking meetings.

You're creating the first impression of Lio. You're opening conversations with the world's largest enterprises and helping procurement leaders understand how AI can transform the way they operate.

You'll work closely with founders, Account Executives, Marketing, and Product, and directly influence how we scale our go-to-market motion.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Generate and qualify pipeline through creative outbound and inbound strategies

 - Engage early-stage prospects and educate them on how AI agents can transform procurement operations

 - Run outreach across multiple channels including email, LinkedIn, and phone

 - Collaborate closely with Marketing and Account Executives to refine messaging, qualification, and handoff processes

 - Capture customer feedback and share insights with Product to help shape future features and improvements

 - Own your pipeline from first touch to handoff and ensure a seamless customer experience

 - Help build and continuously improve Lio's go-to-market playbook


YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Take ownership by default. You don't wait for instructions or permission. You make things happen, care about outcomes, and see challenges as problems to solve rather than obstacles to accept.

 - Know how to communicate and excite. Whether you're speaking to a Fortune 500 executive, a procurement leader, or an operational buyer on the factory floor, you know how to build trust, create excitement, and adapt your message to the person in front of you.

 - Enjoy high-growth environments. Things move quickly, priorities change, and ambiguity doesn't scare you. You learn fast, adapt quickly, and thrive when there's a lot to build.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about the opportunities this technology creates for enterprises. You don't need to be an expert, but you believe AI will fundamentally reshape how businesses operate.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

Previous experience in enterprise sales, business development, or SDR roles is not required.

We're much more interested in ambition, curiosity, communication skills, and execution than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Silicon Valley's most prestigious investor. We're bringing that same ambition, speed, and culture to Munich. This is a unique opportunity to join a company with Silicon Valley DNA while building in Europe.

We work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, working with C-level procurement leaders on large-scale transformation initiatives. From your very first day, you'll be exposed to some of the most influential enterprises in the world and play a role in how AI gets adopted in the real world.

We move fast, iterate constantly, and care deeply about execution. There are no endless approval processes, no politics, and no waiting around for permission. People here own outcomes and continuously raise the bar.

Joining Lio today means joining early. The category is still being defined. The playbook is still being written. Everyone at Lio is expected to take ownership, move fast, and help shape not only the business, but also the culture we're building.

If you're excited about building something exceptional, working with world-class customers, and helping shape one of Europe's category defining AI companies, we'd love to hear from you.



Opportunities like this don't come around often. If you're looking for a comfortable 9-to-5, Lio is probably not for you. If you're excited about building something

source_jobs.title: Solution Architect (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

As AI agents become capable of executing increasingly complex workflows, enterprise buying decisions are becoming increasingly technical. Customers don't just want to hear what's possible—they want to see it working.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As a Soltion Architect, you're not just supporting sales.

You're helping some of the world's largest enterprises gain the technical confidence to adopt AI. You'll build prototypes, lead technical workshops, solve customer challenges, and work side-by-side with our Account Executives to turn complex enterprise opportunities into successful deals.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Own the technical strategy of enterprise opportunities from first conversation to signed deal

 - Lead technical discovery sessions, workshops, and product evaluations with DAX companies and Fortune 500 customers

 - Build prototypes, proof of concepts, and custom extensions that demonstrate measurable business value

 - Design scalable solutions that integrate Lio into complex enterprise environments

 - Build trusted relationships with procurement leaders, IT teams, architects, and C-level stakeholders

 - Work closely with Product and Engineering to turn customer requirements into scalable platform capabilities

 - Help shape our enterprise sales strategy by combining technical expertise with commercial thinking


YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Enjoy combining technology and customer interaction. You like building software just as much as presenting it to customers.

 - Have experience with APIs, SaaS platforms, cloud environments, or software development. You don't need to be a full-time engineer, but you're comfortable building and adapting technical solutions.

 - Take ownership. You enjoy influencing enterprise buying decisions and helping close complex technical deals.

 - Communicate confidently. Whether you're speaking with engineers, architects, procurement teams, or C-level executives, you know how to explain complex technology with clarity.

 - Thrive in fast-moving environments. You enjoy solving new challenges, learning quickly, and working across Sales, Product, and Engineering.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about helping enterprises adopt a new generation of software.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

You don't need experience in procurement or deep knowledge of ERP systems.

We're much more interested in technical curiosity, customer empathy, ownership, and your ability to solve complex problems than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) and built at Y Combinator. We're bringing Silicon Valley ambition and speed to Munich while building one of Europe's leading AI companies.

From day one, you'll work on strategic enterprise opportunities with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US. You'll help shape how AI is adopted inside global enterprises while working closely with Account Executives, Product, Engineering, and our founders.

If you're excited about combining software, enterprise sales, and AI to help win category-defining customers, we'd love to hear from you.

Any questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Founders Associate - CEO Office (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just supporting leadership; you’re amplifying the impact of the CEO and shaping the initiatives that drive Lio’s growth. As a Founders Associate Intern in the CEO Office, you’ll work hands-on with the Go-to-Market team and across the company to turn strategic decisions into action.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Assist the CEO with strategic projects and high-impact decision-making

 - Contribute to go-to-market initiatives, helping launch and optimize campaigns that fuel growth

 - Support the optimization of internal processes to make the company faster, smarter, and more scalable

 - Collaborate closely with Product, Growth, and Sales teams to align cross-functional efforts

 - Take ownership of projects end-to-end, ensuring they move from planning to measurable outcomes




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Are available for a 6-month internship starting in July 2026

 - Have 1+ years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, or high-growth startups

 - Think analytically, act proactively, and turn insights into action

 - Thrive in fast-paced, dynamic environments and adapt quickly to changing priorities

 - Communicate clearly and influence stakeholders across teams, including executives




WHY JOIN US?

At Lio, you’ll work side-by-side with the CEO and leadership team, gaining unparalleled exposure to the strategy and operations of a category-defining AI company. You’ll own projects with real impact, shape go-to-market initiatives, and help build the processes that drive enterprise AI forward. Move fast, own your impact, and be part of a team where every contribution matters. Are you in?

source_jobs.title: AI Implementation Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

For decades, procurement teams have relied on fragmented systems, manual processes, and disconnected workflows. As AI agents become capable of executing increasingly complex tasks, successful implementation becomes just as important as the technology itself.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As an AI Implementation Manager, you're not just managing projects.

You're helping some of the world's largest enterprises successfully adopt AI Agents in their day-to-day procurement operations. You'll lead implementations from kick-off to go-live, work closely with customers and internal teams, and ensure every deployment creates measurable business value.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Own end-to-end enterprise implementations from kick-off to successful go-live

 - Translate customer requirements into scalable implementation plans and solution designs

 - Act as the primary project owner and trusted advisor for DAX and Fortune 500 customers

 - Coordinate Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to ensure seamless execution

 - Proactively manage timelines, risks, stakeholder communication, and escalations

 - Turn customer feedback into actionable product improvements

 - Build scalable implementation processes and playbooks that enable future growth




YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Have experience in software implementations, customer success, implementation consulting, or technical project management.

 - Take ownership by default. You enjoy driving projects from start to finish and making customers successful.

 - Communicate with confidence. You build trust with enterprise customers and coordinate effectively across technical and business teams.

 - Stay structured under complexity. Managing multiple stakeholders, timelines, and priorities is something you enjoy.

 - Think in solutions. You understand customer challenges and translate them into scalable implementation approaches.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about helping enterprises adopt a new generation of software.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

You don't need previous procurement experience or deep technical expertise.

We're much more interested in ownership, structured thinking, customer empathy, and your ability to deliver successful enterprise implementations than in having the perfect background

.


WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) and built at Y Combinator. We're bringing Silicon Valley ambition and speed to Munich while building one of Europe's leading AI companies.

From day one, you'll work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, helping bring AI into real enterprise operations. You'll have real ownership, work closely with Product and Engineering, and directly influence how our platform evolves through customer feedback.

If you're excited about solving complex customer challenges and helping shape the future of enterprise AI, we'd love to hear from you.



Any questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

For decades, procurement teams have relied on spreadsheets, emails, and fragmented processes to manage billions of dollars in spend. As AI agents become capable of executing increasingly complex workflows, enterprise buying decisions are becoming increasingly technical.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As an Account Executive, you're not just closing deals.

You're helping some of the world's largest enterprises understand how AI can transform the way they operate. You'll identify operational challenges, uncover high-impact AI use cases, and work closely with Product and Engineering to design solutions that create measurable business value.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Own complex enterprise opportunities from first conversation to signed agreement

 - Identify customer pain points and uncover high-impact AI use cases across enterprise procurement organizations

 - Lead strategic discovery sessions, workshops, and solution discussions with procurement, IT, and executive stakeholders

 - Translate customer challenges into compelling business value and scalable solution concepts

 - Partner closely with Product and Engineering to solve complex customer problems and shape the future of our platform

 - Navigate complex buying processes across multiple stakeholders and build long-term customer relationships

 - Capture customer feedback and help turn recurring requirements into scalable product capabilities




YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Know how to identify customer problems. You ask thoughtful questions, quickly understand operational challenges, and uncover where technology can create meaningful value.

 - Communicate value, not just features. You connect technical capabilities with business outcomes and help customers build confidence in a new way of working.

 - Bring experience in enterprise software sales, technical sales, consulting, venture capital, IT consulting, product management, or another customer-facing role involving complex B2B solutions. You're comfortable working with sophisticated customers and navigating complex business and technical discussions.

 - Enjoy working closely with Engineering. You like collaborating across technical and commercial teams to solve complex customer challenges.

 - Take ownership by default. You drive opportunities end-to-end and are accountable for outcomes.

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-growth environments and are excited about helping enterprises adopt AI.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

You don't need a Computer Science degree or previous experience in procurement.

We're much more interested in curiosity, analytical thinking, technical affinity, commercial instinct, and your ability to solve customer problems than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) and built at Y Combinator. We're bringing Silicon Valley ambition and speed to Munich while building one of Europe's leading AI companies.

From day one, you'll work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, helping define how AI is adopted inside global enterprises. You'll work side by side with Product, Engineering, and our founders, solving real customer problems while shaping both our product and our go-to-market strategy.

If you're excited about combining technology, enterprise sales, and AI to build category-defining customer relationships, we'd love to hear from you.

Any questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Solution Architect (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just designing architectures; you’re turning enterprise AI deals into real, scalable implementations. As a Solution Architect at Lio, you ensure our AI platform is securely and seamlessly embedded into complex enterprise IT landscapes, transforming signed contracts into long-term customer success.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Build prototypes, proof of concepts, and custom extensions that demonstrate real customer value

 - Configure and adapt Lio's AI platform to customer workflows, ERP systems, and enterprise environments

 - Lead technical deep dives, live demos, and workshops with procurement, IT, and architecture teams

 - Work closely with enterprise customers to understand technical requirements and design scalable solutions

 - Partner with Sales throughout the entire deal cycle as the technical owner of customer success

 - Translate recurring customer needs into scalable product capabilities

 - Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering to continuously improve our platform

 - Travel to customer sites for workshops and technical sessions (1–2 days per week)


YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Have experience building software or integrating technical systems. You're comfortable working with APIs, SaaS platforms, cloud environments, or modern development frameworks.

 - Take ownership by default. You enjoy solving customer problems and turning ideas into working software.

 - Communicate with confidence. You're comfortable explaining technical concepts to both engineers and business stakeholders.

 - Enjoy working with customers. You like understanding real-world challenges and building solutions that create measurable value.

 - Learn quickly. You thrive in fast-moving environments and enjoy working across Sales, Product, and Engineering.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about helping enterprises adopt a new generation of software.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

You don't need experience in procurement or deep knowledge of ERP systems.

We're much more interested in technical curiosity, ownership, strong engineering fundamentals, and your ability to solve customer problems than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN US?

Join Lio and architect how AI Agents operate inside some of the world’s largest enterprises. You’ll work at the intersection of Sales, Product, and Engineering, directly influencing revenue and long-term customer success. From day one, you’ll own high-impact deals, shape real enterprise architectures, and see your designs live in production. Are you in?



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Security, Risk & Compliance Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just managing compliance; you’re building the foundation of trust and security at Lio. As our first hire in this area, you’ll own the implementation and management of security, privacy, and compliance frameworks, ensuring our enterprise AI platform meets the highest standards and instills confidence in global customers.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Lead the implementation and maintenance of security and compliance frameworks, including ISO 27001, SOC2 (upcoming), GDPR, the EU AI Act and others as the company scales

 - Define policies, processes, and controls to protect company and customer data

 - Conduct risk assessments, audits, and gap analyses to proactively identify and mitigate security risks

 - Collaborate with Engineering, Product, and Operations to integrate security and compliance into daily workflows

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for internal and external audits, regulators, and partners

 - Continuously monitor and improve Lio’s security posture as the organization grows




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have proven experience in GRC, security, and compliance management, ideally in SaaS or enterprise software environments

 - Are familiar with ISO 27001, SOC2, GDPR, and other security/compliance frameworks

 - Think strategically and can translate regulatory requirements into practical processes and controls

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-impact environments and can work independently as the first hire in the function

 - Communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders, including executives




WHY JOIN US?

We’re hiring a Security, Risk & Compliance Manager to build Lio’s security and compliance function from the ground up. Lead ISO 27001, SOC2, GDPR, and other initiatives, define policies and controls, and ensure our enterprise AI platform meets the highest standards of trust and safety for global customers.



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Gabriel https://www.linkedin.com/in/gabriel-koch-executive-search/!

source_jobs.title: Founding GTM & Branding Operator (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just building a website; you’re shaping how Lio is perceived by the world.

 

As Founding GTM & Branding Operator, you will work at the intersection of Brand, Marketing, Product, and Growth to define how one of the fastest-growing AI companies communicates, looks, feels, and converts. You’ll work directly with our leadership team and help turn Lio into a category-defining company through exceptional storytelling, design, and execution.

This is a role for someone with exceptional taste, strong business intuition, and a desire to build. Someone who understands that the best brands aren’t created through marketing campaigns — they’re built through consistency, narrative, and attention to detail.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Own and evolve Lio’s brand, positioning, and visual presence

 - Drive the development of our website as a key growth and conversion channel

 - Translate complex AI topics into compelling stories and visuals

 - Create presentations, landing pages, campaigns, event materials, and other brand assets

 - Build scalable templates, guidelines, and systems that enable teams across Lio

 - Work closely with founders, agencies, and stakeholders to ensure brand consistency across all touchpoints

 - Support product launches, GTM initiatives, recruiting, and company-building efforts




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have a strong portfolio showcasing a variety of projects, brands, and styles

 - Bring exceptional taste and attention to detail

 - Can execute and elevate existing visions, not just your own ideas

 - Understand storytelling, positioning, and conversion—not just design

 - Are highly proactive, hands-on, and thrive in fast-paced startup environments

 - Are experienced with Figma, PowerPoint, and Adobe Illustrator or Affinity Designer

 - Admire brands like Stripe, Linear, OpenAI, Vercel, Ramp, or Clay




WHY JOIN US?

You’ll help define how one of the fastest-growing AI companies is experienced by customers, candidates, and the market. High ownership, direct founder collaboration, and the opportunity to build a category-defining brand from the ground up.



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Anika https://www.linkedin.com/in/anika-holinski-2849b4129/!

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

As AI agents become capable of executing increasingly complex workflows, enterprise customers expect more than presentations—they want to see working solutions inside their own environments.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As a Sales Engineer, you're not just supporting sales.

You're helping some of the world's largest enterprises understand what's technically possible with AI. You'll build prototypes, lead technical workshops, and work closely with customers to turn complex requirements into working software that proves real business value.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Build prototypes, proof-of-concepts, and custom solutions that demonstrate the value of our platform

 - Configure and adapt Lio's AI platform to customer workflows and enterprise system landscapes

 - Lead technical deep dives, live demos, and workshops with procurement, IT, and architecture teams

 - Partner closely with Sales throughout the entire enterprise sales process as the technical expert

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering to translate recurring customer needs into scalable product capabilities

 - Travel to customer sites for workshops and technical sessions (1–2 times per week)

 - Help shape how enterprise customers adopt AI in complex production environments


YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Have experience building software. You're comfortable working with APIs, integrations, cloud platforms, or modern development frameworks.

 - Enjoy solving customer problems. You like understanding technical requirements and turning them into working solutions.

 - Take ownership by default. You move quickly, build pragmatically, and care about delivering measurable customer value.

 - Communicate confidently. You're comfortable presenting technical concepts to engineers, IT teams, and senior stakeholders alike.

 - Enjoy working across disciplines. You like collaborating with Sales, Product, Engineering, and customers to make complex projects successful.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about bringing cutting-edge technology into enterprise environments.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

You don't need experience in procurement software or every technology in our stack.

We're much more interested in strong technical fundamentals, curiosity, ownership, and your ability to solve customer problems than in checking every box on a technology list.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) and built at Y Combinator. We're bringing Silicon Valley ambition and speed to Munich while building one of Europe's leading AI companies.

From day one, you'll work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, helping some of the world's largest enterprises adopt AI. You'll build real solutions, influence our product through customer feedback, and work closely with founders, Sales, Product, and Engineering to shape how enterprise AI is deployed.

If you're excited about combining engineering, customer interaction, and cutting-edge AI, we'd love to hear from you.

Any questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Finance Intern (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
Finance Intern

As a Finance Intern at Lio, you will play a role in building and scaling the finance operations of a fast-growing AI company. Backed by a16z and Y Combinator, we are building a category-defining product. Working closely with the Finance team and collaborating across departments, you will help design and execute processes, reporting structures, and records that you build with us to scale and automate as we grow.

What You'll Do

In this role, you will:

 - Help build the foundation of a state-of-the-art finance operations function — designing scalable processes from the ground up

 - Support accounts receivable processes, including tracking outstanding invoices and ensuring timely collection

 - Take ownership of invoice documentation and filing, maintaining accurate and audit-ready financial records

 - Contribute to the preparation of investor reporting, supporting both quantitative analysis and the accompanying commentary

 - Identify opportunities to automate repetitive finance workflows using AI-powered tools, helping us move faster and smarter as we scale

 - Support ad-hoc projects across finance and revenue operations, including data analysis and one-off requests that inform strategic decisions

Your Profile

You are a strong fit for this role if you:

 - Are available for a minimum of 4 months starting as soon as possible

 - Are currently pursuing or have recently completed a degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or a related discipline

 - Possess strong Excel skills and a structured, detail-oriented approach to your work — experience with modern finance tools or automation platforms is a plus

 - Are genuinely excited about the intersection of finance and technology, and curious about how AI can transform financial operations

 - Demonstrate analytical thinking, proactivity, and the ability to translate complex inputs into clear outputs

 - Adapt well to fast-paced, dynamic environments and shifting priorities

 - Communicate fluently in English; proficiency in German is an advantage

Why Join Us?

At Lio, you won't just support finance — you'll help build it. This is a rare opportunity to get in early at a high-growth AI company and leave a real mark on the systems and processes that will power our next stage of growth. You'll work alongside senior leadership, gain hands-on exposure to startup finance and investor reporting, and develop a toolkit that combines financial rigor with modern, AI-driven ways of working.

Are you in?

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
FORWARD DEPLOYED ENGINEER (ALL GENDERS)




THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

As AI agents become capable of executing increasingly complex workflows, enterprise software needs to integrate seamlessly with existing systems. That's where you come in.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you'll work at the intersection of Engineering, Product, and enterprise customers. You'll build integrations, solve complex technical challenges, and help turn customer requirements into scalable product capabilities.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Build enterprise integrations across APIs, SAP, and other business systems

 - Work directly with enterprise customers to design scalable technical solutions

 - Debug and resolve complex issues across production environments

 - Own customer-driven product features from implementation to production

 - Build MVPs and evolve them into scalable platform capabilities

 - Automate deployment and implementation workflows with Product and Engineering

 - Turn recurring customer needs into reusable product features
   
   


YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Enjoy solving technical problems across APIs, integrations, and distributed systems.

 - Take ownership. You care about building reliable, scalable solutions—not just getting things to work.

 - Like working closely with customers and translating real-world challenges into product improvements.

 - Communicate clearly and enjoy collaborating across Engineering, Product, and customer teams.

 - Enjoy fast-moving environments where you can learn quickly and have a direct impact.

 - Are excited about AI and the opportunity to bring it into enterprise operations.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

Experience with procurement software or SAP isn't required.

We're much more interested in strong engineering fundamentals, curiosity, ownership, and your ability to solve complex technical challenges than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) and built at Y Combinator. We're bringing Silicon Valley ambition and engineering culture to Munich.

From day one, you'll work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, solving real-world technical challenges at the intersection of AI and enterprise software.

We move fast, care deeply about execution, and give engineers ownership from day one. The solutions you build won't stay customer-specific—they'll shape the future of our platform.

If you're excited about building AI systems that run in production at global enterprises, we'd love to hear from you.

Any questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Cansel https://www.linkedin.com/in/cansel-bayram-6a422420b/.

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just supporting sales; you’re making enterprise AI deals technically undeniable. As a Sales Engineer at askLio, you turn customer requirements into working software, proving real-world value inside complex enterprise environments.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Build prototypes, proof-of-concepts, and custom extensions that demonstrate value

 - Configure and adapt Lio’s AI-driven P2P platform to customer workflows and ERP landscapes

 - Lead technical deep dives, live demos, and workshops with procurement, IT, and architecture teams

 - Travel to customer sites for workshops (1–2 times per week)

 - Translate recurring customer needs into scalable product features

 - Partner with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Founders as the technical owner of deal success




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have hands-on experience with SaaS systems, APIs, cloud architectures, and data-driven applications

 - Can build, extend, or integrate software (Python/FastAPI, React/TypeScript, REST APIs, MongoDB, or similar)

 - Are comfortable working in enterprise IT landscapes and cloud platforms (e.g., Azure)

 - Take ownership, thrive in dynamic environments, and prefer shipping working software over slide decks

 - Enjoy collaborating closely with customers and C-level stakeholders




WHY JOIN US?

Join Lio and be at the forefront of AI-powered procurement. You’ll own high-impact deals, shape product features, and see your solutions live inside the world’s largest companies. Fast-paced, high-ownership, and customer-facing. If you want your technical work to make a real, measurable impact, this is the place. Are you in?



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Gabriel https://www.linkedin.com/in/gabriel-koch-executive-search/!

source_jobs.title: Core Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just writing code; you’re building the foundation of Lio’s enterprise AI platform. Your work will power our SaaS and Agent products, making them scalable, reliable, and impactful for global customers. You’ll help translate real-world customer needs into robust technical solutions that drive measurable value.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Design and implement agent-based architectures and workflows

 - Build and scale high-performance cloud SaaS systems across backend and frontend

 - Turn product and customer requirements into production-ready solutions

 - Own initiatives end-to-end, from architecture to deployment

 - Occasionally interact with customers to clarify needs and ensure successful delivery

 - Continuously improve code quality and engineering standards




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have 5+ years of experience as a Software Engineer in cloud SaaS applications, with strong expertise in Python, TypeScript, Java, or similar languages OR are a recent Master’s graduate from a top university (TUM, Oxford, Ivy League, ETH, KIT, etc.) with hands-on development experience through internships, working student positions, or personal projects

 - Are passionate about writing clean, maintainable code and building products that delight users

 - Think end-to-end: from system design to production-ready implementation

 - Thrive in a fast-paced, high-impact environment and take ownership of critical technical decisions

 - Can communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

source_jobs.title: Open Application (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
Don’t see the perfect role listed? We’re always looking for exceptional people who want to build the future of AI-driven procurement with us. If you believe you could add value to Lio, we’d love to hear from you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Tell us how you would contribute to Lio. Whether it’s product, engineering, sales, operations, or something entirely new: we’re excited to learn about your ideas, experience, and ambitions.




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Are excited about building and scaling an AI company

 - Thrive in fast-paced startup environments

 - Want to have real impact from day one




WHY JOIN US?

At Lio, you’ll work with a highly ambitious team building a category-defining AI company. If you’re driven, curious, and ready to move fast: we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate.

For decades, procurement teams have relied on spreadsheets, emails, and fragmented processes to manage billions of dollars in spend. As AI agents become capable of taking over increasingly complex workflows, the way companies run procurement is about to change.

At Lio, we're building THE AI workforce for procurement.

As a Sales Development Representative, you're not just booking meetings.

You're creating the first impression of Lio. You're opening conversations with the world's largest enterprises and helping procurement leaders understand how AI can transform the way they operate.

You'll work closely with founders, Account Executives, Marketing, and Product, and directly influence how we scale our go-to-market motion.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Generate and qualify pipeline through creative outbound and inbound strategies

 - Engage early-stage prospects and educate them on how AI agents can transform procurement operations

 - Run outreach across multiple channels including email, LinkedIn, and phone

 - Collaborate closely with Marketing and Account Executives to refine messaging, qualification, and handoff processes

 - Capture customer feedback and share insights with Product to help shape future features and improvements

 - Own your pipeline from first touch to handoff and ensure a seamless customer experience

 - Help build and continuously improve Lio's go-to-market playbook




YOUR PROFILE

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Take ownership by default. You don't wait for instructions or permission. You make things happen, care about outcomes, and see challenges as problems to solve rather than obstacles to accept.

 - Know how to communicate and excite. Whether you're speaking to a Fortune 500 executive, a procurement leader, or an operational buyer on the factory floor, you know how to build trust, create excitement, and adapt your message to the person in front of you.

 - Enjoy high-growth environments. Things move quickly, priorities change, and ambiguity doesn't scare you. You learn fast, adapt quickly, and thrive when there's a lot to build.

 - Are curious about AI and excited about the opportunities this technology creates for enterprises. You don't need to be an expert, but you believe AI will fundamentally reshape how businesses operate.


WHAT'S NOT REQUIRED

Previous experience in enterprise sales, business development, or SDR roles is not required.

We're much more interested in ambition, curiosity, communication skills, and execution than in having the perfect CV.




WHY JOIN LIO?

Lio is backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Silicon Valley's most prestigious investor. We're bringing that same ambition, speed, and culture to Munich. This is a unique opportunity to join a company with Silicon Valley DNA while building in Europe.

We work with Fortune 500 companies across Europe and the US, working with C-level procurement leaders on large-scale transformation initiatives. From your very first day, you'll be exposed to some of the most influential enterprises in the world and play a role in how AI gets adopted in the real world.

We move fast, iterate constantly, and care deeply about execution. There are no endless approval processes, no politics, and no waiting around for permission. People here own outcomes and continuously raise the bar.

Joining Lio today means joining early. The category is still being defined. The playbook is still being written. Everyone at Lio is expected to take ownership, move fast, and help shape not only the business, but also the culture we're building.

If you're excited about building something exceptional, working with world-class customers, and helping shape one of Europe's category defining AI companies, we'd love to hear from you.



Opportunities like this don't come around often. If you're looking for a comfortable 9-to-5, Lio is probably not for you. If you're excited about building somethi

source_jobs.title: IT Administration & Automation Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just managing IT; you’re automating it. As IT Automation & Systems Manager at Lio, you replace manual processes with smart workflows, build internal tools that outperform SaaS solutions, and create a secure, scalable IT foundation for a fast-growing AI company.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Automate onboarding, offboarding, access management, and device provisioning across our tool stack

 - Build lightweight internal tools (low-code / vibe-coded / custom scripts) to replace or optimize SaaS workflows

 - Integrate and orchestrate systems into a clean, automated setup

 - Own role- and group-based access structures and continuously improve permission management

 - Support IT security initiatives such as firewall setup, device policies, and security best practices

 - Document systems clearly and create scalable IT processes that grow with the company




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have experience in IT administration, systems engineering, or IT operations in a startup or SaaS environment

 - Think automation-first and prefer building systems over manually executing tasks

 - Can script or build internal tools (e.g., via Python, APIs, low-code tools, or AI-assisted coding)

 - Understand identity & access management, SaaS ecosystems, and modern cloud environments

 - Care about security fundamentals and structured permission concepts

 - Work independently, pragmatically, and solution-oriented




WHY JOIN US?

At Lio, IT is not a support function, it’s a leverage function. You’ll design an automation-first IT landscape, eliminate repetitive work, and build internal systems that scale with our growth. Your work will directly increase efficiency, security, and speed across the company. Move fast, automate everything, and help power a category-defining AI company. Are you in?



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Gabriel https://www.linkedin.com/in/gabriel-koch-executive-search/!

source_jobs.title: Head of US Sales (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
You’re not just entering a market; you’re scaling it. As Head of US Sales at Lio, you build on our first US enterprise wins and turn early traction into a scalable, high-performing revenue engine while creating a strong bridge between New York and our Munich HQ.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

In this role, you will:

 - Scale Lio’s US go-to-market strategy from initial deals to repeatable, predictable growth

 - Expand and deepen relationships with flagship enterprise customers

 - Hire, lead, and develop a high-performing US sales team aligned with our HQ values and culture

 - Build a strong executive network across enterprise procurement and AI ecosystems

 - Work closely with Munich (Sales, Product, Marketing, Leadership) to ensure tight transatlantic alignment

 - Bring structured US market feedback into product, positioning, and strategy decisions




YOUR PROFILE

You’re a good fit for this role if you:

 - Have 5–10+ years of experience in enterprise SaaS sales, including leadership responsibility

 - Have scaled a US market presence beyond the first deals into structured growth

 - Combine hands-on closing strength with strategic market-building thinking

 - Are well-connected in the US enterprise or tech ecosystem

 - Lead by example and build strong, value-driven cultures

 - Thrive in fast-paced, high-ownership environments




WHY JOIN US?

At Lio, you’ll build on real momentum; not a blank slate. You’ll scale our US footprint, shape the team, and turn early enterprise traction into long-term market leadership. Together with our Munich HQ, you’ll create a tightly aligned transatlantic growth engine for a category-defining AI company. Are you in?



Have questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Anika https://www.linkedin.com/in/anika-holinski-2849b4129/!

notes: High volume of sales and sales engineering roles, with a smaller but still significant number of engineering and product roles. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
